title: Add `--log-level` command line option and deprecate `--debug`
body:
The previous log-level implementation does not seem to work, which is
now fixed and aligns to other projects like podman and CRI-O.

Added documentation and integration testing as well.

comments.body:
I think if we are going to do this, we should remove (hide) `--debug` flag, since `--log-level debug` is the same thing, and becomes the default command users should use.

They should conflict, but removing --debug from man pages and hiding it from the CLI, is required to add --log-level

@saschagrunert when you said log-level didn't work, you just mean that the command wasn't there to do something like '--log-level=debug' right?  But the `--debug` flag is working as expected right?

Confession time, I hate the `--log-level` option, I much prefer the bare `--debug`.  But that being said, I'd be fine with this change as long as we keep --debug.  I'm prefer not hiding --debug, but am OK if it is hidden.  As long as my little crutch doesn't disappear!

> @saschagrunert when you said log-level didn't work, you just mean that the command wasn't there to do something like '--log-level=debug' right? But the `--debug` flag is working as expected right?

Yes, something like `--log-level=...` does currently not work because the flag is not registered. `--debug` works as intended right now.

> Confession time, I hate the `--log-level` option, I much prefer the bare `--debug`. But that being said, I'd be fine with this change as long as we keep --debug. I'm prefer not hiding --debug, but am OK if it is hidden. As long as my little crutch doesn't disappear!

I mean we could also remove `--log-level` completely to only stick to `--debug`. But then we would not be able to show only warnings or info messages via logrus.
I think we should move to log-level to be consistent with Podman and to allow the flexibility that @saschagrunert just pointed out.
Alright this is now a bit larger than expected, so let's see if the tests are passing.

> Just wanted to verify that the --debug option works as it did before, it's just not advertised. Is that correct @saschagruneret?

Yes, this is the intended behavior. :+1:

@TomSweeneyRedHat I tested it out, and everything seems to work fine.
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit d94fd87 has been approved by `rhatdan`
